'''Simon James Haughton''' (born 10 November 1975) is an English former professional [[rugby league]] and [[rugby union]] footballer who played as a {{rlp|sr}} forward in the 1990s and 2000s. He played representative level [[rugby league]] for [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]] and [[England national rugby league team|England]], and at club level for the [[Wigan Warriors]] and [[Oldham RLFC]],<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/> and club level [[rugby union]] for [[Orrell R.U.F.C.]].

==Early life==
Haughton was born in [[Bingley]], [[West Yorkshire, England]],<ref>{{cite web|last1=Wilson|first1=Andy|title=Walker gives another four years to Rhinos|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2001/jun/20/rugbyleague.andywilson|website=The Guardian|accessdate=17 July 2017|date=20 June 2001}}</ref> and attended [[Bingley Grammar School]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Yorkshire’s oldest schools with some dating back to the seventh and eighth centuries including Kings School in Pontefract, The Minster School in York and St Peter’s School |url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/heritage-and-retro/heritage/the-history-of-yorkshires-oldest-schools-dating-back-to-the-seventh-and-eighth-centuries-including-the-minster-school-york-and-beverley-grammar-school-4036109 |website=Yorkshire Post |access-date=21 January 2024 |date=21 February 2023}}</ref> He grew up playing [[rugby league]] for amateur club Dudley Hill, signing a professional contract with [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] on his 17th birthday.<ref>{{cite web|last1=Woods|first1=Dave|title=Haughton on his way out|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/super_league/1974934.stm|website=BBC Sport|access-date=17 July 2017|date=8 May 2002}}</ref>

==Playing career==
===Club career===
Haughton made his debut in November 1993 in the second round of the [[1993–94 Regal Trophy]], scoring a try in a 22–8 win against [[Whitehaven R.L.F.C.|Whitehaven]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Rugby League: Crusaders' cruise |url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/rugby-league-crusaders-cruise-1504376.html |website=The Independent |access-date=21 January 2024 |date=15 November 1993}}</ref>

He appeared as a substitute in the [[1994–95 Rugby League Premiership|1995 Premiership]] final, scoring a try in the 69–12 win over [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Radlinski rampant in Wigan rout |url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/radlinski-rampant-in-wigan-rout-1620684.html |website=The Independent |access-date=21 January 2024 |date=21 May 1995}}</ref>

Haughton played {{rlp|lf}} in [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]]'s 25–16 victory over [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] in the [[1995–96 Regal Trophy]] Final at [[John Smith's Stadium|Alfred McAlpine Stadium]], [[Huddersfield]] on Saturday 13 January 1996.<ref name="13th January 1996: St Helens 16 Wigan 25 (Regal Trophy Final)">{{cite web|url=http://wigan.rlfans.com/news.php?readmore=2976|title=13th January 1996: St Helens 16 Wigan 25 (Regal Trophy Final)|publisher=wigan.rlfans.com|date=31 December 2014|accessdate=1 January 2015|archive-date=7 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181007005311/http://wigan.rlfans.com/news.php?readmore=2976|url-status=dead}}</ref>

He played for Wigan Warriors from the interchange bench in their [[1998 Super League Grand Final]] victory over [[Leeds Rhinos]].

In July 2002, Haughton switched codes to sign for [[rugby union]] side [[Orrell R.U.F.C.|Orrell]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Haughton joins Orrell |url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/super_league/2132148.stm |website=BBC Sport |access-date=12 June 2022 |date=16 July 2002}}</ref> He returned to rugby league in December 2004, signing for [[Oldham R.L.F.C.|Oldham]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Simon is set for Oldham |url=https://www.manchestereveningnews.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/simon-is-set-for-oldham-1132605 |website=Manchester Evening News |access-date=12 June 2022 |date=17 December 2004}}</ref>

===International honours===
Haughton was an [[England national rugby league team|England]] international and debuted for the team at the [[1995 Rugby League World Cup]], making four appearances during the tournament. He made two further appearances for England in 1999 against [[France national rugby league team|France]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Sport: Rugby League  French foiled by England fightback |url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/2/hi/sport/rugby_league/474290.stm |website=BBC News |access-date=21 January 2024 |date=13 October 1999}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |title=Sport: Rugby League  England rack up half-century |url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/1/hi/sport/rugby_league/483691.stm |website=BBC News |access-date=21 January 2024 |date=23 October 1999}}</ref> He was also selected for the [[2000 Rugby League World Cup|2000 World Cup]], but withdrew from the squad.<ref>{{cite web |title=England replace Hay with Haughton |url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/935648.stm |website=BBC Sport |access-date=20 January 2024 |date=21 September 2000}}</ref>

In the 1997 post-season Haughton was selected to play for [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]] in the [[Super League Test series]] against Australia, scoring two tries in the third and deciding match.<ref>{{cite web |title=Australia see off Lions challenge |url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/1/hi/sport/31878.stm |website=BBC News |access-date=21 January 2024 |date=17 November 1997}}</ref>

==Post-playing==
After retiring from rugby, Haughton emigrated to Australia with his family.<ref>{{cite web |title=Haughton now calls Top End home |url=https://www.ntnews.com.au/sport/rugby-union/haughton-now-calls-top-end-home/news-story/5ffee71399eb9a738a6195664e0eea15 |website=NT News |access-date=21 January 2024 |date=19 April 2015}}</ref>
